name: Restore accepted base, then retime refill issue order

summary.name: Restore accepted base, then retime refill issue order
summary.hypothesis: Anchor on the latest measured run 20260420_085928_bf16_gemm_v1_9ee9b48 at 25.821696 ms, restore the accepted grouped_rows=8 base with reversed PTX row-pair traversal, right-left PTX column sweep, and the one-sync wait_group_0 handoff, then try a narrow steady-state refill issue-order retime inside that accepted base. The mirrored-sweep experiment already reduced long scoreboard to 4.48 but still regressed badly, so consumer ordering is no longer the best next lever.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Refill issue ordering after the accepted one-sync handoff, not the consumer sweep order itself.
summary.risk: Low-to-moderate: if the accepted base is not fully restored first, a refill retime can confound the signal and drift back into the rejected mirrored or split-sweep paths.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the accepted grouped_rows=8 base is restored but a local refill issue-order retime does not improve time without reopening the rejected mirrored or split-sweep branches.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
